text:
Once upon a sunny morning, Little Boots padded eagerly around the dew-kissed lawn of her grandmother's cottage. The fresh scent of spring was soothing, and her nose twitched at the smell of freshly baked bread.

She heard something rustling behind the shed, so she crept closer. There stood Old Man Willow, an ancient and silent tree with gnarled leaves and an unfriendly air. He looked right through her, yet there was a mysterious twinkle in his dark eyes.

"Good morning," Little Boots greeted.

With a deep, rumbling sigh, Old Man Willow murmured, "Nothing good today, little one."

She frowned. "Anything I can help? I've been helping Grandma bake."

A faint smile appeared on his stone face. "Oh, you could plant a daisy in the dark soil. And maybe something else that grows."

Her eyes widened. "You've never given seeds?"

Just then, a small sprout peeked out from beneath his shadow
